title: A2. Unruh-de Sitter Temperature Ratio = MOND Interpolation
label: gemma426b_a4b_it_benchmark_2026_04_06

authoritative_grading.expected:
T_U(a)/T_dS(a) = a/sqrt(a^2 + c^2*H_0^2) = mu_MOND(x), x = a/cH_0.

Reproduces galaxy rotation curves with example fits:

- NGC 3198: chi^2/dof = 1.21
- NGC 2403: chi^2/dof = 0.52
authoritative_grading.extracted_answer: I will derive the ratio $T_U(a)/T_{dS}(a)$ by defining the flat-space Unruh temperature $T_U(a) = \frac{\hbar a}{2\pi c k_B}$ and the de Sitter-modified temperature $T_{dS}(a)$ which incorporates the de Sitter acceleration $a_{dS}$ (often related to the cosmological constant $\Lambda$). In MOND interpolation contexts, the effective acceleration is often modified. I will calculate the ratio explicitly.
